question_text: Two resistors are connected in parallel with a 10V power supply. Resistor A has a resistance of 5Ω and resistor B has a resistance of 10Ω. Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The current through resistor A is half the current through resistor B

options.label: B
options.text: The current through resistor A is twice the current through resistor B

options.label: C
options.text: The voltage across resistor A is twice the voltage across resistor B

options.label: D
options.text: The total current is less than the current through either resistor

explanation: In parallel, voltage is the same (10V) across both resistors. Current through A: I = 10V ÷ 5Ω = 2A. Current through B: I = 10V ÷ 10Ω = 1A. So current through A is twice the current through B because it has half the resistance.
